meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no version control (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Master professional version control and portfolio building. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to transform your raw code into a deployable, high-impact project.
content.answer_block: Version control is the backbone of professional engineering. By integrating Git-based workflows into your portfolio building process, you transform scattered files into a structured, trackable history. Using Antigravity to automate repository scaffolding and Claude Code to manage iterative commits, you ensure every line of code adds tangible value to your portfolio.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding, but your project exists only as a chaotic folder of local files. This isn't just poor organization; it’s a career-limiting bottleneck. When you lack version control, you lack a professional story, and recruiters can't see the evolution of your expertise. 

Today, we stop building in the dark. We are going to treat your code as a high-stakes product. By leveraging Antigravity to architect your repository and Claude Code to enforce rigorous commit standards, you will turn your raw project into a professional-grade portfolio piece that demonstrates technical maturity and engineering discipline.

steps.title: Architecting the Repository Structure
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, establish a professional directory hierarchy that signals competence to any hiring manager.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional project boilerplate with standard folder structures: /src, /tests, /docs, and /scripts. Include a README.md, .gitignore for Python/Node.js, and a LICENSE file.

steps.title: Initializing Version Control with Purpose
steps.description: Transform your local folder into a Git repository. This establishes the 'source of truth' for your project development history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new git repository in the current directory and create an initial commit with a standard semantic message.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude commit -m 'feat: initialize project structure'

steps.title: Automating Commit Hygiene
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce Conventional Commits, ensuring your commit history tells a clear, chronological story of your development process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file changes and generate a conventional commit message that describes the technical impact of these modifications.

steps.title: Documenting Technical Debt and Decisions
steps.description: A great portfolio isn't just code; it's the documentation of your decision-making process. Create an ADR (Architecture Decision Record).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an ADR for the current project, documenting why we chose the selected tech stack and how it solves the problem of version control.

steps.title: Deployment as Portfolio Proof
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI/CD pipeline to prove your code is not just functional, but deployable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs tests on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Never commit 'WIP' messages; use Claude Code to rebase and squash your commits into meaningful feature milestones before pushing.
Treat your README.md as a product landing page; include a 'How to Run' section that is foolproof for a non-technical recruiter.
Use Git tags to mark version milestones (v1.0, v2.0) to demonstrate that you understand software release lifecycles.
Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your repository documentation to highlight the specific engineering challenges you overcame.
Automate your .gitignore file to prevent sensitive environment variables from ever touching your repository history.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your repository is live, deploy your project to a hosting provider like Vercel or Railway. Update your LinkedIn profile with the repository link and write a short post detailing one specific technical hurdle you solved during this build.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your best code rot in a local folder. Build a portfolio that gets you hired by mastering the tools the pros use. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
